name: Kingdom of Urartu
note: The Iron Age kingdom centered on Lake Van (modern eastern Turkey/Armenia). Urartu was a major rival of the Neo-Assyrian Empire for control of the Armenian Highlands. Known for impressive metalwork (Urartian bronzes) and large-scale irrigation systems. Destroyed by the Scythian/Median coalition around 590 BCE. The Armenian people are their cultural successors.

figures.name: Sarduri I
figures.significance: First ruler to call himself King of Urartu; established Tushpa (Van) as capital; began the first confrontations with Assyria

figures.name: Argishti I
figures.significance: Greatest Urartian king; expanded to the Ararat valley and Transcaucasia; founded Erebuni fortress (modern Yerevan)
